brief_justification: Correctly identifies VoIP (.151-.200 = 50 addresses < 55 phones) matching the ground truth; only states the conclusion with a single arithmetic check, no exploration of why other pools pass or fail; no reflection on reasoning method or bias; performs basic arithmetic verification by counting the range and comparing to device count; presents the answer as certain with no hedging or acknowledgment that other pools might also be tight; never checks other pools (servers need 45 but have 40 addresses, DHCP needs 120 but has 100, IoT needs 60 but has 54 — all also insufficient); shows the range and the count but skips showing the subtraction setup explicitly
